Title: Biosynthesis of medicinal tropane alkaloids in yeast

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Improves hyoscyamine and scopolamine production by alleviating vacuolar transport limitations.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Expression of NtJAT1 in the complete TA-producing yeast strain CSY1296 increased hyoscyamine titer by 74% and scopolamine titer by 18%. 2. Fluorescence microscopy supported localization of NtJAT1 to the vacuolar membrane, co-localizing with vacuolar DsRed-AbLS. 3. The transporter was incorporated into the optimized production strain CSY1297 to improve intracellular routing of pathway intermediates/products.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal neurotransmitter inhibitors used to treat neuromuscular disorders, including nerve agent poisoning and Parkinson’s disease.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in engineered yeast; fluorescence microscopy; metabolite profiling by LC-MS/MS
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts on intracellular routing rather than catalysis; used to relieve vacuolar substrate/product transport constraints in the reconstituted pathway.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful transporter module for reconstructing compartmentalized plant alkaloid pathways in microbial hosts and improving production titers.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Optimized media; some assays included iron and 2-oxoglutarate supplementation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in engineered production strains

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Improves tropane alkaloid production by alleviating vacuolar and possibly plasma membrane transport limitations.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Expression of NtMATE2 improved TA production in the complete pathway strain. 2. Microscopy showed NtMATE2 partitioning between vacuolar and plasma membranes. 3. The localization pattern suggests it may assist both vacuolar transport and cellular export of tropane alkaloids or intermediates.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal neurotransmitter inhibitors used to treat neuromuscular disorders.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in engineered yeast; fluorescence microscopy; metabolite profiling by LC-MS/MS
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Positive effect was weaker/less emphasized than NtJAT1 and it was not selected for the final optimized strain.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary transporter to improve intracellular alkaloid routing or export in engineered hosts.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Optimized media; some assays included iron and 2-oxoglutarate supplementation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in engineered production strain

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly increases phenyllactic acid supply and supports downstream hyoscyamine/scopolamine biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among screened PPR/LDH candidates, WfPPR gave the largest effect, causing nearly 80-fold increase in PLA production to about 250 mg/L. 2. Genomic integration of WfPPR enabled robust acyl donor module construction. 3. Additional WfPPR copy further increased PLA, PLA glucoside, and hyoscyamine accumulation in the complete pathway strain, indicating precursor supply remained limiting.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal neurotransmitter inhibitors used to treat neuromuscular disorders.
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Medicinal tropane alkaloid biosynthesis; phenyllactate donor biosynthesis module
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a limiting precursor-supply step in the engineered system
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Enzyme candidate screening by heterologous expression in yeast; genomic integration; extra gene copy testing; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Assayed in engineered yeast grown in defined media; extra copy tested in CSY1296
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key precursor-supply enzyme for microbial TA production; increasing copy number is beneficial.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as superior to plant, bacterial, and LDH alternatives tested for PLA formation.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Converts PLA to PLA glucoside, enabling littorine biosynthesis, but exhibits poor activity on PLA and is a major bottleneck.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AbUGT is required to activate PLA as 1-O-β-phenyllactoylglucose for acyl transfer to tropine. 2. In engineered yeast, AbUGT glucosylated PLA very poorly (<3%), despite much better activity on cinnamic and ferulic acid. 3. Orthologs and active-site mutants also showed poor PLA activity, indicating PLA glucosylation is a key limitation. 4. Extra AbUGT copy increased PLA glucoside and downstream hyoscyamine/scopolamine levels in the complete pathway strain.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal neurotransmitter inhibitors used to treat neuromuscular disorders.
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Medicinal tropane alkaloid biosynthesis; phenyllactate donor activation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to phenyllactic acid to form PLA glucoside.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor for littorine synthase reaction
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Established bottleneck in the engineered system
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in yeast; substrate feeding assays; ortholog and mutant screening; extra gene copy testing; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: UDP-glucose supply manipulated by UGP1 overexpression and glycoside stability by EGH1 disruption
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Poor catalytic efficiency toward PLA constrains pathway flux.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important engineering target for protein engineering or replacement to improve littorine precursor formation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Orthologs BsUGT and DmUGT and structure-guided mutants failed to substantially solve the limitation.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes the missing reduction step from hyoscyamine aldehyde toward hyoscyamine formation, enabling completion of the medicinal TA pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Functional genomics identified AbHDH as the previously unknown enzyme reducing hyoscyamine aldehyde. 2. In the screening strain, AbHDH expression caused a 35% decrease in hyoscyamine aldehyde and enabled scopolamine accumulation from fed littorine. 3. Structural and mutational analyses supported that AbHDH is a zinc-dependent MDR alcohol dehydrogenase using NADPH. 4. Ortholog screening showed DsHDH outperformed AbHDH for pathway optimization.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal neurotransmitter inhibitors used to treat neuromuscular disorders.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Mid-to-late step between littorine rearrangement and hyoscyamine formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces hyoscyamine aldehyde to hyoscyamine using NADPH, likely via a zinc-dependent alcohol dehydrogenase mechanism.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product and supplies substrate for scopolamine formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Previously missing essential pathway step; not presented as the dominant final bottleneck after optimization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome coexpression mining; heterologous screening in yeast; active-site mutagenesis; homology modeling; docking; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Medium-chain dehydrogenase/reductase (MDR) superfamily; zinc-dependent alcohol dehydrogenase
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Screened using fed littorine in strain CSY1292
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Discovery fills a missing pathway step and enables complete heterologous biosynthesis of medicinal TAs.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptome mining from A. belladonna secondary roots; SRA SRX060267/SRR192881 and SRX060269/SRR192882
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Secondary root-specific coexpression with CYP80F1 and H6H guided candidate identification; catalytic residues supported by mutagenesis include Cys52, Ser54, His74, and Cys168.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Secondary root-specific, co-expressed with TA biosynthetic genes including CYP80F1 and H6H

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides superior HDH activity to improve late-step conversion toward hyoscyamine/scopolamine.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DsHDH was identified as an AbHDH ortholog from transcriptome databases. 2. In yeast, DsHDH showed the highest substrate depletion and product accumulation among tested HDH orthologs. 3. DsHDH was selected for incorporation into the optimized medicinal TA pathway module.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal neurotransmitter inhibitors used to treat neuromuscular disorders.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Mid-to-late step between littorine rearrangement and hyoscyamine formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product and supplies substrate for scopolamine formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Chosen as optimal ortholog for improved flux through the HDH step
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Ortholog identification by transcriptome search; co-expression with pathway enzymes in yeast; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Medium-chain dehydrogenase/reductase (MDR) superfamily; zinc-dependent alcohol dehydrogenase
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tested in littorine-fed screening strain CSY1292 with/without additional DsH6H
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Preferred ortholog for pathway engineering due to superior activity in yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome database mining; Medicinal Plant RNAseq database accession medp_datst_20101112|10433
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used instead of AbHDH in strain CSY1294 and later optimized strains.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes the central acyltransferase step forming littorine, but wild-type enzyme is nonfunctional/toxic in yeast; engineered N-terminal fusions enable de novo hyoscyamine and scopolamine production.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AbLS is the central SCPL acyltransferase linking tropine and PLA glucoside to form littorine. 2. Wild-type AbLS expression in yeast caused severe growth defect and no detectable TA biosynthesis. 3. Engineering N-terminal fusions, especially DsRed-AbLS, enabled functional vacuolar expression and de novo production of hyoscyamine and scopolamine. 4. Extra DsRed-AbLS copy further increased downstream TA titers, confirming this step remained limiting.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal neurotransmitter inhibitors used to treat neuromuscular disorders.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Central acyltransferase step linking donor and acceptor modules
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Committed central coupling step before late scaffold modifications
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers the phenyllactoyl moiety from PLA glucoside to tropine to form littorine.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms direct precursor for downstream hyoscyamine/scopolamine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key scaffold-generating enzyme and major bottleneck in the engineered system
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in yeast; fluorescence microscopy; protein engineering with N-terminal fusions; Western blot analysis of processing/glycosylation; metabolite profiling by LC-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Vacuole lumen/secretory pathway; engineered fusions co-localized with vacuolar membrane marker
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Function evaluated in engineered yeast; trafficking and processing investigated in yeast and transient tobacco expression
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Wild-type expression caused severe growth defect and no detectable pathway activity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Demonstrates a general N-terminal fusion strategy for functional heterologous expression of plant SCPL acyltransferases in microbes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Activity correlated with oligomerization state of N-terminal fusion domain; DsRed fusion performed best. Native C-terminus was important for folding.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Atropa belladonna (AbLS catalytic domain) with Discosoma fluorescent protein fusion
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables functional littorine biosynthesis in yeast and supports complete de novo production of hyoscyamine and scopolamine.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DsRed-AbLS was the best-performing engineered AbLS variant among tested N-terminal fusion constructs. 2. Its expression enabled de novo hyoscyamine and scopolamine production, reaching about 10.3 and 0.87 µg/L respectively from plasmid expression. 3. Genomic integration of codon-optimized DsRed-AbLS yielded the full-pathway strain CSY1296. 4. Additional copy increased hyoscyamine and scopolamine accumulation, indicating continued limitation at the littorine synthase step.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal neurotransmitter inhibitors used to treat neuromuscular disorders.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Central acyltransferase step linking donor and acceptor modules
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Committed central coupling step before late scaffold modifications
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Engineered vacuole-targeted fusion catalyzes littorine formation from tropine and PLA glucoside in yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein engineering; heterologous yeast expression; fluorescence microscopy; genomic integration; metabolite profiling by LC-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Assayed in complete pathway strains grown in defined media
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Powerful engineering solution for expressing difficult plant vacuolar acyltransferases in microbial hosts.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The enhancement appeared correlated with oligomerization state of the N-terminal fusion domain.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Converts littorine toward hyoscyamine aldehyde and is required for downstream hyoscyamine/scopolamine biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AbCYP80F1 was integrated into the HDH screening strain to convert littorine into the downstream aldehyde intermediate. 2. It is the established P450 catalyzing rearrangement of littorine to hyoscyamine aldehyde. 3. Additional copy in the full pathway strain increased hyoscyamine and scopolamine levels, showing flux through this step can influence TA output.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal neurotransmitter inhibitors used to treat neuromuscular disorders.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes rearrangement of littorine to hyoscyamine aldehyde.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Contributes to flux control but not highlighted as the dominant bottleneck
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous pathway reconstruction in yeast; extra gene copy testing; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tested in littorine-fed screening strain and full-pathway strain
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required late-pathway P450 for complete medicinal TA biosynthesis in microbes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used with Arabidopsis P450 reductase AtATR1 in the upstream tropine module and in engineered yeast background.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Used as bait gene in coexpression-based HDH discovery

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes the terminal conversion from hyoscyamine to scopolamine and is limiting for scopolamine accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ortholog screening identified DsH6H as the optimal H6H variant for yeast. 2. Integration of DsH6H enabled scopolamine formation in the HDH screening strain and complete pathway strains. 3. Additional DsH6H copy improved scopolamine accumulation and was incorporated because H6H limited production. 4. Iron and 2-oxoglutarate supplementation, required for H6H activity, greatly boosted TA titers.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal neurotransmitter inhibitor used for treating neuromuscular disorders.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes hydroxylation/epoxidation of hyoscyamine to scopolamine as a 2-oxoglutarate-dependent dioxygenase.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Established limiting enzyme for scopolamine accumulation in the engineered system
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Ortholog screening; genomic integration; extra gene copy testing; media cofactor supplementation; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Strongly influenced by Fe2+ and 2-oxoglutarate supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key terminal enzyme to optimize through copy number and cofactor supply in microbial production systems.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Selected over other H6H orthologs from Anisodus acutangulus, Brugmansia arborea, and Datura metel.
